ground_truth.title: Newsroom - ADHA
ground_truth.main_content: Welcome to the Newsroom. The American Dental Hygienists' Association (ADHA®) is the largest and oldest organization representing the professional interests of the more than 220,000 dental hygienists in the United States. Dental hygienists are preventive oral health professionals, licensed in dental hygiene, who provide educational, clinical and therapeutic services that support total health through the promotion of optimal oral health. Read news and announcements involving membership, leadership, education, sponsorship, events, the dental hygiene profession and the ADHA Foundation. For media inquiries or questions on news and announcements involving the ADHA or the ADHA Foundation, please contact [email protected]. The newsroom contains press releases and announcements organized by year including: ADHA Opposes Exclusion of Dental Hygiene from Degree Definition; ADHA Issues Statement Opposing Department of Education's Exclusion of Dental Hygiene from Professional Degree Definition; ADHA Announces 2025 Award Recipients; Statement Regarding ADHA's Participation in the Xylitol Petition to the FDA; Catherine K. Draper Elevated to Editor in Chief of the Journal of Dental Hygiene; ADHA President Honors Oral Health Leaders; The American Dental Hygienists' Association Announces 2025 Class of Professional Fellows; American Dental Hygienists' Association Rebrands Podcast to "Hygiene Rising™"; ADHA Board Approves New Policies at Long Beach Meeting; PDS Health and ADHA Collaborate to Expand Access to Dental Care to ADHA Members; ADHA White Paper Calls for Dental Hygienist Practice Expansion and Autonomy to Address Access Crisis; New Interactive Map Streamlines Licensure Guidance for Dental Hygienists Nationwide; ADHA Modernizes Governance Structure, Transitions from House of Delegates; ADHA Partners with Andau Medical to Advance Ergonomic Solutions for Dental Hygienists; Statement From American Dental Hygienists' Association on Enactment of H.R. 1; American Dental Hygienists' Association Announces New Leadership for 2025-2026; Institute for Oral Health Rebrands as ADHA Foundation; ADHA Names Jennifer Hill as Chief Executive Officer; ADHA Opposes Arizona Senate Bill 1037 Amendment That Eliminates Educational Standards for Oral Preventive Assistants; and many more announcements and media coverage from external sources.

Dental hygienists are preventive oral health professionals, licensed in dental hygiene, who provide educational, clinical and therapeutic services that support total health through the promotion of optimal oral health.
